← All paddles

Gherkin

Wraith S

All-CourtWidebodyUSAP approved2025Intermediate

2025 widebody all-court paddle from Gherkin, with a 16mm core, a soft, dense feel and very high spin.

$135

Street price as of July 2026 — retailers move it.

Search Gherkin — Gherkin Wraith S (opens in new tab)

This link goes straight to the brand's own site — we don't earn a commission from it today. If that changes, we'll say so right here and on our disclosure page.

Measured performance.

Where this paddle sits against the 514 we track. We describe the band it lands in rather than publishing the underlying lab figures — those are licensed, and the bands are the part we can share.

SpinVery highUpper range

How much bite the face puts on the ball.

PowerVery highUpper range

Ball speed off the face on a full swing.

ControlHighMiddle of the catalog

Touch and placement — the trade-off against raw power.

ForgivenessHighMiddle of the catalog

How stable the head stays on an off-centre hit.

Hand speedVery highTop fifth of the catalog

How quickly it moves in a hands battle at the kitchen.

Full specifications

BrandGherkin
Released2025
Tournament approvalUSAP
ShapeWidebody
Impact feelSoft/Dense
Core thickness16 mm
Static weight7.96 oz
Balance point232 mm
Grip length5.5 in
Grip circumference4.25 in
Spin ratingVery High
Best forIntermediate

Where to go from here.

Same play style, a different trade-off — one steps up the measured performance, one protects your wallet.

Top pick

Adidas PRO-EDT 16 C

$189 +$54 vs this

The step up in this play style: more spin · more forgiving · slower through the air. Same widebody shape, 16mm core.

See this paddle →
Value pick

Friday Fever 2

$99 −$36 vs this

Keeps the all-court character for less: less power · less spin · more forgiving. Same widebody shape, 16mm core.

See this paddle →

Similar paddles.

The closest six in the catalog on shape, core, balance and measured character — not just the same brand.
